recommendation: Codify a neutral-judge-prompt rule in gate-validation-governance: a producer/judge iteration loop must not let judge prompts accumulate leading language round over round, and a stalled loop (multiple judge-iteration rounds relocating rather than resolving the same defect) should trigger a neutral-prompt re-run before continuing to iterate.
evidence: send-submit-realign: 8 consecutive judge-iteration rounds (round 1-8) each reframed a contested key-naming justification into new prose without resolving it, per the requester's own scope-correction diagnosis: "the judge prompts had grown progressively more leading, which manufactured findings rather than surfacing them"

send-submit-realign round 9, run with a NEUTRAL prompt after the requester stopped the loop, immediately found a material contract defect all 8 leading rounds missed: the headline invariant that only the submit verb presses Enter is false and unachievable under the chosen design

recommendation: Add a named failure mode to gate-validation-governance / combat-log-governance's cause guidance for iteration loops where a producer's fix relocates a judge-flagged defect into new scope or new prose rather than repairing it, and have the conductor treat repeated relocation (not merely repeated rejection) as a stronger stop-and-rescope signal than an ordinary judge-iteration count.
evidence: 11-layout-walk-ceiling: cause "contradiction-relocated-not-repaired" (the round-1 fix moved the pairwise scenario contradiction rather than repairing it) and cause "measurement-over-read-second-time" (the same over-reading defect class recurring a second time)

send-submit-realign: 8 consecutive judge-iteration rounds each relocating the same naming-justification defect into new prose before the requester manually broke the loop (see the sibling entry distilled from this mission)
the relocate-not-repair shape recurs across the two richest correction histories in the corpus (11-layout-walk-ceiling, send-submit-realign) out of 7 missions carrying a combat log

recommendation: Extend the scenario-bridge tooling (sdd:verify-scenarios / sdd:check-scenario-overlap) to detect near-miss title matches: when a scenario reports UNBOUND, check whether its normalized title matches an EXTRA (unclaimed) test title differing only by punctuation or whitespace variants (e.g. a curly vs straight apostrophe), and report that as a probable title-mismatch rather than silently listing a false UNBOUND alongside its own near-duplicate in the EXTRA list.
evidence: 21-bind-pane-scenarios followup: a mux-node scenario was reported UNBOUND for a reason unrelated to issue #21's own diagnosis — its already-verbatim-bound test title differed from the suite only by a curly vs straight apostrophe, so the identical string appeared in both the UNBOUND and EXTRA lists with nothing cross-referencing them
the mission's own followup names this "the same class of failure as the unparseable-suite bug already filed for this project: a real signal rendered invisible"

recommendation: The correction `cause` field is not populated consistently across this project's combat logs, which undercuts recurring-pattern detection (contracted to read a matchable cause). Recommend the Council pick one: (a) grow sdd:combat-log-governance's cause enum with categories this project's own history has already surfaced as real and recurring (e.g. a short slug for the relocate-not-repair shape in entry seq 2, and one for judge-prompt drift in seq 1), or (b) tighten spec-producer/impl-producer guidance so every correction line writes cause as a short matchable slug, not a free sentence, in every mission rather than only some.
evidence: cause-as-short-slug: 11-layout-walk-ceiling, all 7 correction lines (e.g. "contradiction-relocated-not-repaired", "sibling-spec-goes-stale")

cause-as-full-sentence, none matching sdd:combat-log-governance's 4-value discovered enum (coverage-gap / design-overreach / spec-feature-contradiction / prose-impl-contradiction): send-submit-realign (11/11 lines), 8-layout-save, 5-layout-templates, 9-open-reports-workspace

this Scanner had to hand-read correction detail text to group the seq-2 recurring pattern by hand rather than by counting a matchable field, which is exactly the cost this project's own doctrine loop is meant to avoid

recommendation: The six frozen provision scenarios narrate a CLI invocation ("Given a caller running cyber-mux worktree provision …") for a verb the CLI never wires — provision ships only as the library seam provisionWorktree / WorktreeApi.provision, and the worktree-provision ADR itself defers the CLI verb as a follow-up. Recommend suite-format-governance require a Scenario's Given to name the surface actually shipped (library function vs CLI verb) so a frozen scenario cannot assert a CLI verb the implementation does not provide; alternatively re-altitude these six scenarios to the library seam they truly verify.
evidence: worktree.feature: all 6 provision scenarios open with "Given a caller running cyber-mux worktree provision …", but src/cli.ts wires only worktree add/open/list/remove — no provision verb exists
design/decisions worktree-provision ADR: "dryRun was NOT added … Left as a clean follow-up if a CLI worktree provision verb ever wants one" — the CLI verb is unshipped by design
the impl gate approved a frozen suite phrased at a CLI altitude the shipped CLI does not reach — a spec-feature-contradiction the gate did not surface

recommendation: A shipped mission (worktree provision/acquire, issue #79, merged as #80/#82) left no ledger provenance — no leash line, no gate line, and no combat-log shard exists for it — so the doctrine loop cannot detect-and-draft from it by contract; this Scanner located it only by reading git log, not the ledger. Recommend the Council decide whether every mission reaching → implemented must carry at least a gate line, and if so add a guard (check-project-specs / the retirement sweep) that flags a spec-corpus change with no matching ledger shard, so the loop's own input is never silently missing.
evidence: ledger/ holds a shard for every other CR (10-split-options-contract … 63-worktree-list-table-markers, send-submit-realign) but none for worktree-provision
worktree README + worktree.feature + the worktree-provision ADR all landed the capability, yet no leash/gate line records the mission
the last ledger-touching commit predates the provision commits, confirming the mission bypassed the ledger entirely

recommendation: Codify an axis-generalization check for spec-producers and the spec-judge: when a rule holds across a surface that varies along an axis (verb, backend, route), a scenario pinned to one point while the rule is stated generally is a coverage-gap, and answering each instance by enumerating points scenario-by-scenario is the patch-per-route anti-pattern — the fix belongs at the seam that owns the rule. Also require the spec-judge be briefed on every node a rule spans BEFORE the first round: a single-node brief inherits the producer's blind spot.
evidence: 19-env-flag: the same one-route-false-on-the-other defect recurred five times across the verb and backend axes (cause: coverage-gap x6, spec-feature-contradiction x5, design-overreach x3)
rounds one-through-four were each briefed on one node and inherited that blind spot; the requester stopped a fifth judge and required a self-review, which found what four rounds had not
seven spec-gate rounds before the first builder PASS — the cost of patching per route instead of generalizing at the seam

recommendation: Codify a discriminating-backend rule in suite/impl governance: a test bound to a frozen scenario must be authored on the backend that can OBSERVE the behavior under test. tmux collapses the workspace and tab tiers onto one new-window, so a placement or label assertion there emits identical argv at either tier and stays green under mutation — a false-green bind that verifies nothing. Bind such scenarios on the backend where the tiers are distinct.
evidence: 14-template-tabs: three would-have-shipped defects of one pattern — a tab-label/placement test written on tmux survived mutation because the identical argv is emitted at either tier (cause: false-green-test, would-have-shipped x2)

two conductor-error lines: a self-imposed seam limit reported to the requester as an external constraint, and an invented label-uniqueness rule — both dissolved once probed against live tmux/herdr rather than argued

recommendation: Codify two measurement-discipline rules for explore-phase empirical spikes: (a) a volatile measured fact belongs in ONE durable home, never duplicated verbatim across a node README, a design doc, and the spec — hand-maintenance drifts the copies; and (b) a generalization drawn from spike data must be checked against its own datapoints before it enters the spec, since an averaged summary can be refuted by the table it summarizes.
evidence: 11-layout-walk-ceiling: the spike measurement was over-read twice (cause: measurement-generalization-refuted-by-own-data, measurement-over-read-second-time) — a claimed per-pane floor its own table refuted at two of four datapoints

cause volatile-fact-duplicated-across-durable-homes — the table, probe, and portability note were each carried verbatim in both the README and the design doc while the spec called the numbers the backend's to change
cause sibling-spec-goes-stale, contradiction-relocated-not-repaired (cross-ref strategy seq 2)

recommendation: Codify a false-tense-resists-spot-fix rule: a claim written in deliver-tense about a contract not yet met survives a local reword by merely changing tense — the repair must re-altitude the claim to what the contract actually guarantees and be checked at the call site, not at the restatement. Have the spec-judge treat a tense edit to a previously-flagged false-tense claim as unresolved until the call-site check passes.
evidence: 36-axi-error-surface: the false-tense class survived by changing tense — the spot-fix pattern recurred round over round; a consumer node contradicted the suite being frozen
matches the project's recurring false-tense/doc-sync failure across #36 and #40

recommendation: Codify an unverified-claim gate: a rationale that gates a decision (an exit-code choice, an edit-class classification) must be probed against the real tool before it is allowed to gate — not reasoned from memory. Recurring unverified claims here rested on facts never checked until a judge forced the probe.
evidence: 31-address-pane-by-name: cause unverified-claim-caught x3 — the exit-code rationale rested on a fact never checked, and the structural edit-class check ran against the wrong subject
a reference node written in false-built tense recurred after a first correction; a conductor wrote correction lines to the wrong ledger shard
aligns with the project's standing probe-before-declaring-a-blocker lesson

recommendation: Codify that the conductor's own mechanical edits at a gate (an apparatus swap) are subject to re-judging: a spec-gate apparatus swap here introduced a self-inflicted contradiction a fresh judge round had to catch. Also flag apparatus-absorption (a scenario's apparatus quietly carrying the behavior under test) as a named spec-judge finding.
evidence: 5-layout-templates: cause self-inflicted-contradiction — the conductor's mechanical apparatus swap at the spec gate introduced a contradiction; two apparatus-absorption findings
cause unverified-claim-caught — an --env flag emitted on herdr's worktree route that cannot carry it, the same one-route defect class that later dominated 19-env-flag (cross-ref seq 7)

recommendation: Codify the false-bind impl-judge check: a test whose title claims a frozen scenario but whose assertions cannot fail the behavior is a false bind — it must be caught by mutation/oracle-appropriate checking, not by title-match. Three impl-judge rounds were spent surfacing exactly this.
evidence: 8-layout-save: three cold impl-judge rounds returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false; cause self-caught — a test bound to a frozen captured-template scenario verified nothing until repaired
same false-bind class as 14-template-tabs' false-green tests (cross-ref seq 8)

recommendation: Codify a design-of-record coverage-claim check: when the design of record asserts scenarios cover a behavior, that claim must be verified against the actual frozen suite before the gate — here the design claimed tab/workspace-ratio scenarios that were not present.
evidence: 10-split-options-contract: cause self-caught — the design of record claimed new tab/workspace-ratio scenarios the suite did not hold; an impl-judge found a herdr test bound to the wrong subject

recommendation: Codify that a frozen suite which fails to parse must fail structural-freeze verification LOUDLY, never silently skip it: the gherkin did not parse here and freeze verification could not run, risking an unguarded freeze. The parse check should be a hard precondition of the spec gate.
evidence: 9-open-reports-workspace: cause frozen-suite-repair — structural freeze verification could not run because the gherkin did not parse; a cold impl-judge separately found the added mux scenarios mis-phrased; a requester scope-correction on a bare-open surface gap
same unparseable-suite-hides-signal class as strategy seq 3 and 56-spec-corpus-drift (cross-ref seq 16)

recommendation: Codify run-the-engine-not-a-substitute: a hand-glob or title-set comparison that reports green still misses what the real engine surfaces. A scenario map here was textually complete but unparseable to the lint (rows recognized only when backtick-wrapped), and a title-set compare called it green while every row was invisible; the discovery engine also surfaced a project-name finding the hand-glob missed.
evidence: 56-spec-corpus-drift: a scenario map landed textually complete but unparseable to the lint; a title-set comparison called it green while every row was invisible, caught only by running the suite check
hand-globbing for the project spec missed the name-resolution finding the discovery engine surfaced
the project's standing run-the-engine-not-a-hand-glob lesson; cross-ref strategy seq 3

recommendation: Codify that a human-vs-structured behavioral boundary must be pinned at the SURFACE, not at a CLI flag: pinning it at --format json is hollowed out the moment a format default (e.g. an owed TOON default) changes. Also: scenario literals (marker glyphs) must live in the frozen suite, not only in a never-frozen README, or the thing the scenario exists to pin is unprotected.
evidence: 63-worktree-list-table-markers: spec-judge builder lens caught three underdetermination defects — a Then on a sibling-prefix path the Given never built, marker literals living only in the never-frozen README, and the human-vs-structured boundary pinned at the --format json flag rather than the surface (cause: judge-iteration x2)

recommendation: Codify a formation-split hygiene step: after relocating a scenario to a new unit spec, grep the source for comments referencing that scenario's subject and repoint any still naming the pre-split owner — a stale source comment survived the split here until a text grep caught it.
evidence: 58-split-oversized-nodes: a five-way formation split relocated both suites cleanly (verified against pre-move hashed title sets), but left one stale source comment pointing at the pre-split unit, corrected after grepping the scenario text

recommendation: No doctrine change recommended — retro complete. A clean coverage-closing mission: CLI-level tests bound the remaining UNBOUND mux.feature scenarios (two were Scenario Outlines needing a tmux+herdr case each), taking the scenario bridge to full BOUND+PASS with no correction line. Recorded to confirm the distill ran so the plan can retire.
evidence: complete-mux-scenario-coverage: single clean report line, no correction; scenario bridge reached full BOUND+PASS, typecheck clean

recommendation: Extend the scenario-bridge tooling to surface the hoist trap: the bridge stops binding at the first spec: segment, so a scenario whose real binding lives deeper or at a sibling library node is reported UNBOUND even when a covering test exists — flag it as probable hoist-truncation, not a genuine gap. Related: several CLI-surface coverage gaps here were provable only at the library node, so the bridge should follow bindings across the CLI/library seam rather than judging each node in isolation.
evidence: 83-adopt-scenario-bridge-binding: build-to-learn bound the cli/worktree exemplar 24/31; found the hoist trap (bridge stops at first spec: segment) and 7 CLI-surface coverage gaps proven only at the library node
